1.description: Social welfare. Solid line: Social welfare as a function of Sector 1 size K1 using the social-welfare-maximizing emission cap, given K1. Dashed line: Social welfare without emission caps, |$\hat U(1, K_1 , 1 - K_1 )$|⁠, as a function of Sector 1 size K1. Parameters: |$\gamma _1 = \gamma _2 = \alpha _1 = \alpha _2 = 0.5$|⁠, δ = 6.

2.description: SRF’s performance as a function of the SRF’s size S and strategy. panel a) Mere exclusion strategy where |$K_2^{FB} : = 1 - K^O$|⁠, panel b) Scope 1 strategy, panel c) upstream Scope 3 strategy. Parameters are: |$\gamma _1 = \gamma _2 = \alpha _1 = \alpha _2 = 0.5$|⁠, δ = 6, |$\psi = 0.025, \;\lambda = 0.5$|⁠.

3.description: SRF size and impact as a function of the fraction θ of value-alignment investors. Three strategies: mere exclusion (solid lines), Scope 1 (dotted line), and Upstream Scope 3 (dashed line). These graphs are for δ = 6, |$\mu = 0.005$|⁠, and |$\psi = 0.025, \;\lambda = 0.5$| with capital market frictions in both sectors. Furthermore, for panels a) and b), |$\alpha _1 = \alpha _2 = \gamma _1 = \gamma _2 = 0.5$|⁠; for panels c) and d), |$\alpha _1 = 0.1, \alpha _2 = 0.8, \gamma _1 = 0.2, \gamma _2 = 0.8$|⁠.

4.description: SRF size and impact. Size (panel a) and impact (panel b) as a function of the fraction θ of value-alignment investors for three strategies: mere exclusion (Solid lines), Scope 1 (dashed line), Upstream Scope 3 (dotted line), Downtream Scope 3 (gray dashed line), and Degrowth (gray solid line). These graphs are obtained for |$\alpha _1 = \alpha _2 = \gamma _1 = \gamma _2 = \lambda = 0.5$|⁠, δ = 6, |$\mu = 0.005$|⁠, and |$\psi = 0.025, \;\lambda = 0.5, \;\rho = 0.05$| with capital market frictions in both sectors.
